content.boundary: Applies to primary schools (grades 1-4/1-6) in settlements under 2,000 inhabitants currently under tankerületi/Klebelsberg Center maintenance authority; does not alter funding formulas, governance ownership, or secondary education.
content.change_lever: Statutory procedural requirement (national legislation/decree) mandating documented alternatives assessment, community/municipal consultation, and published rationale before any closure or suspension decision, with a formal appeal channel.
content.name: Legally codified presumption-against-closure with mandatory alternatives assessment
content.system_problem: School closure/suspension decisions in Hungary are made administratively by tankerületi centers via 'temporary closure' or 'suspension of educational activity' without a dedicated statutory closure procedure, formal alternatives assessment, or codified consultation/appeal mechanism, leaving closures driven by efficiency/enrollment ratios alone and reversible only through informal political intervention after public protest.
